disclosure.reason: Public benchmark docs disclose outcomes and reproducibility boundaries while keeping non-public materials out of public artifacts.

rows.label: LongMemEval full gate
rows.note: Benchmark-specific retrieval metric; not cross-normalized with LoCoMo.

rows.label: LoCoMo current full-dataset gate
rows.scope: 1,982-question hash-bound deterministic post-reader replay with zero execution errors
rows.note: Official internal retrieval lock. Full-row post-reader replay, not a fresh provider rerun or E2E J-score. Pipeline warm p95 is inherited from the sealed parent; the actuator adds 0.477 ms p95 locally.

rows.label: LoCoMo tail-repair full gate
rows.scope: 1,982-question retrieval gate with three cold and three warm runs
rows.note: Narrow tail-recall repair: preserves the current full-gate top-15 metrics while improving Any@50 and All@50. Not a new @1/@3 headline.

diagnosticRows.label: LoCoMo current 300-query tuning gate
diagnosticRows.note: Diagnostic tuning slice only. It is intentionally excluded from the public retrieval comparison table and must not be used as a full-dataset replacement row.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo E2E QA policy gate
qaRows.note: Policy score is official-style 1:1: +5.11 points over the accepted adaptive gate and +6.38 points over the matched compact control. Legacy internal pass-rate diagnostics are not public/provider-comparable scoring.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo E2E QA historical stability control, all-category stress score
qaRows.note: Historical three-run all-category control. It is retained for stability context and is not the current QA headline/mean.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo E2E QA stability baseline, core Categories 1-4
qaRows.scope: 3 x 231 core questions within matched deterministic 300-question gates
qaRows.note: Three-run core diagnostic with the full range disclosed. This is a stability baseline, not the default serving path or a same-mode provider row.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo E2E QA stability baseline, adversarial Category 5
qaRows.scope: 3 x 69 adversarial questions within matched deterministic gates
qaRows.note: Robustness lane only; not ordinary answer QA.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo E2E QA all-category stress gate
qaRows.note: Current accepted adaptive gate; +1.27 points over the same-run compact control, +0.43 points over the historical prior gate, and 5.90% lower answer p95 than the matched control. Not a full-dataset or same-mode provider row.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo E2E QA core Categories 1-4
qaRows.scope: 231 core questions within the deterministic 300-question gate
qaRows.note: +1.21 points over the same-run compact control and +1.00 points over the historical prior core slice. Category 5 is reported separately; this is not a same-mode provider row.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo adversarial Category 5 robustness
qaRows.scope: 69 adversarial questions within the deterministic 300-question gate
qaRows.note: Reported separately because Category 5 empty-answer labels do not represent ordinary answer QA.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo E2E QA matched compact control
qaRows.scope: Same 300 questions, retrieval receipt, providers, judge, and run window
qaRows.note: Matched control for isolating the adaptive answer-policy lift from provider/run variance.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo E2E QA prior core Categories 1-4
qaRows.scope: 231 core questions within the historical prior 300-question gate
qaRows.note: Historical prior core slice; not the same-run compact control.

qaRows.label: LoCoMo E2E QA prior gate
qaRows.note: Prior E2E QA gate used for the accepted-gate lift comparison.

benchmarkProtocolDisclosure: LoCoMo and LongMemEval use different evaluation protocols. LoCoMo Any@3 measures exact-turn retrieval across tightly clustered sessions. LongMemEval Any@3 measures answer-cluster retrieval across a larger haystack. Both are reported without cross-benchmark normalization. Small-slice retrieval probes are diagnostic only and are not rendered in the public retrieval comparison table.
qaProtocolDisclosure: LoCoMo end-to-end QA policy scoring is official-style 1:1. Legacy internal pass-rate diagnostics can still be used for product debugging, but they are not policy scores and are not provider-comparable. Provider-style comparison still requires matching dataset revision, included categories, answer model, judge, prompt, retrieval depth, cache state, hardware, and raw artifacts. Core Categories 1-4 and adversarial Category 5 are separate because Category 5 uses empty-answer robustness labels.
qaWeaknessDisclosure: For provider-comparison copy, lead with the LoCoMo QA policy score, which is now identical to the official-style mean. Legacy internal pass-rate diagnostics are intentionally excluded from the public policy table. The older 83.67% all-category three-run figure is a historical stability control, not the current mean. Answer p95 is still model-generation latency: 14.524 s for the policy gate, 14.630 s for the quality stability lane, and 7.047 s for the faster adaptive lane. Retrieval latency is reported separately and remains roughly sub-20 ms on the low-latency memory route.
providerComparisonBoundary: Provider rows are sourced public figures or third-party documentation unless explicitly marked same-mode. Direct comparison requires matched dataset revision, task, metric, answer model, judge, k, cache state, hardware, and raw artifacts.
